title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, a 42-year-old from Braintree, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon for the Patriots Charitable Foundation.
McHugh returned to running after overcoming Stage 3 breast cancer diagnosed in 2023, following treatments and surgeries.
Inspired by late friend Susan Hurley, McHugh resumes marathons to honor friends and prove resilience against cancer.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Sen. Ed Markey questions Meta's plans to use facial recognition in smart glasses, citing privacy risks.
Lawmakers demand Meta explain data collection and storage practices for biometric data by April 6.
Nationwide protests, including 'No Kings' rallies in Massachusetts, are set for March 28.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy city council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million bond to replace firefighting gear containing PFAS.
Mayor Koch's proposal aims to provide PFAS-free gear amid cancer concerns linked to current equipment exposure.
Firefighters express disappointment over the delay, as councilors request more time to review costs and gear durability.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro officials propose turning Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve, citing $57 million in needed repairs and rising costs.
The proposed plan includes free or reduced admission, focusing on native wildlife and rehabilitation, maintaining public access in some form.
Zoo staff express concerns over the transition plan, emphasizing animal care stability and encouraging community feedback on the proposal.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Abigail Brown and Colin Wong held a microwedding in Somerville after a blizzard canceled their original plans.
FX's "Love Story" series highlights 1990s communication, prompting nostalgia for phone-free dinner conversations.
Meredith Goldstein explores the evolving use of the term 'partner' in modern relationships in her newsletter.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, 44, of Burlington, MA, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH.
Averback lost his father to glioblastoma and survived testicular cancer, both treated at Massachusetts General Hospital.
Despite a serious blood infection, Averback's marathon run honors his father and celebrates his own survival.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots safety Kevin Byard is working out with Eagles WR A.J. Brown, heightening trade speculation.
The Eagles may wait until June 1 to trade Brown for salary cap reasons; a first-round pick is sought.
Former Patriots WR Julian Edelman posted a GIF with Brown, fueling further trade rumors around the league.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens concludes his sophomore season at Boston College with 47 points in 34 games.
The Boston Bruins anticipate Hagens may sign his entry-level contract and join the team soon.
Hagens could boost the Bruins' lineup as they aim for playoff contention in the NHL season.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'Is This Thing On?' starring Will Arnett and Laura Dern is now streaming on Disney+.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' featuring Cillian Murphy and Barry Keoghan is available on Netflix.
'Born to Bowl', a new docuseries narrated by Liev Schreiber, is streaming on HBO Max.

title: ‘We’ll see’: Alex Cora mum on future with Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Alex Cora hints at uncertainty about his future with the Red Sox beyond his contract ending in 2027.
Cora expressed contentment with the team's current state and prioritizes his family's happiness in career decisions.
Cora is close to surpassing Terry Francona's win total, needing 134 more victories with the Red Sox.
